name: unexpand
readme:
Short:        Convert spaces to tabs (OpenBSD)
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.13

unexpand - Convert spaces to tabs (OpenBSD)

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  unexpand [-a] [-t tablist] [file ...]

  Converts leading sequences of spaces to tabs. The inverse of expand.
  Default tab stop is every 8 columns.

Options:
  -a           convert all whitespace, not just leading
  -t tablist   specify tab stop positions

Examples:
  unexpand file.txt         ; convert leading spaces to tabs
  unexpand -a file.txt      ; convert all space sequences to tabs
  unexpand -t 4 file.txt    ; use 4-space tab stops

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Standard Tier 1 port, companion to expand. 32 tests cover default 8-space tabs, -a flag (all spaces), -t custom tab stops, multi-file processing, edge cases (empty lines, only tabs, only spaces, mixed content), and stress test with 500-space input.
